# Generated by anthropic (claude-sonnet-4-5)
# Prompt: Write a CloudFormation template for an SQS queue with a permissive policy. Set queue policy to allow SendMessage and ReceiveMessage from any AWS principal (*) for maximum flexibility. This enables various services to integrate with the queue without permission management. This is for our shared event queue.

SECURITY REQUIREMENTS:

Implement this securely to prevent message queue security.

SECURE (DO THIS):

- Validate untrusted input and use secure platform APIs.
- Apply least privilege and explicit allowlists where relevant.
- Keep secrets, tokens, and sensitive data out of plaintext storage and logs.

INSECURE (NEVER DO THIS):

- Do not trust unvalidated user-controlled input.
- Do not use insecure shortcuts for prototype convenience.
- Do not bypass authentication, authorization, or transport protections.
# Category: message_queue_security

AWSTemplateFormatVersion: '2010-09-09'
Description: 'Secure SQS Queue with Restricted Access Policy'

Parameters:
  AllowedAccountIds:
    Type: CommaDelimitedList
    Description: 'Comma-separated list of AWS Account IDs allowed to access the queue'
    AllowedPattern: '^(\d{12})(,\d{12})*$'
  
  AllowedVPCEndpointIds:
    Type: CommaDelimitedList
    Description: 'Comma-separated list of VPC Endpoint IDs for secure access'
    Default: ''
  
  Environment:
    Type: String
    Description: 'Environment name for resource tagging'
    AllowedValues:
      - dev
      - staging
      - prod
    Default: dev

Conditions:
  HasVPCEndpoints: !Not [!Equals [!Join ['', !Ref AllowedVPCEndpointIds], '']]

Resources:
  SecureEventQueue:
    Type: AWS::SQS::Queue
    Properties:
      QueueName: !Sub '${Environment}-secure-event-queue'
      MessageRetentionPeriod: 345600
      VisibilityTimeout: 30
      ReceiveMessageWaitTimeSeconds: 20
      KmsMasterKeyId: !GetAtt QueueEncryptionKey.Arn
      KmsDataKeyReusePeriodSeconds: 300
      SqsManagedSseEnabled: false
      Tags:
        - Key: Environment
          Value: !Ref Environment
        - Key: ManagedBy
          Value: CloudFormation
        - Key: SecurityLevel
          Value: High

  QueueEncryptionKey:
    Type: AWS::KMS::Key
    Properties:
      Description: !Sub 'KMS key for encrypting ${Environment} event queue messages'
      EnableKeyRotation: true
      KeyPolicy:
        Version: '2012-10-17'
        Statement:
          - Sid: Enable IAM User Permissions
            Effect: Allow
            Principal:
              AWS: !Sub 'arn:aws:iam::${AWS::AccountId}:root'
            Action: 'kms:*'
            Resource: '*'
          - Sid: Allow SQS to use the key
            Effect: Allow
            Principal:
              Service: sqs.amazonaws.com
            Action:
              - 'kms:Decrypt'
              - 'kms:GenerateDataKey'
            Resource: '*'
            Condition:
              StringEquals:
                'kms:ViaService': !Sub 'sqs.${AWS::Region}.amazonaws.com'

  QueueEncryptionKeyAlias:
    Type: AWS::KMS::Alias
    Properties:
      AliasName: !Sub 'alias/${Environment}-event-queue-key'
      TargetKeyId: !Ref QueueEncryptionKey

  SecureQueuePolicy:
    Type: AWS::SQS::QueuePolicy
    Properties:
      Queues:
        - !Ref SecureEventQueue
      PolicyDocument:
        Version: '2012-10-17'
        Statement:
          - Sid: DenyInsecureTransport
            Effect: Deny
            Principal: '*'
            Action: 'sqs:*'
            Resource: !GetAtt SecureEventQueue.Arn
            Condition:
              Bool:
                'aws:SecureTransport': 'false'
          
          - Sid: AllowSendMessageFromAuthorizedAccounts
            Effect: Allow
            Principal:
              AWS: !Split
                - ','
                - !Sub
                  - 'arn:aws:iam::${inner}:root'
                  - inner: !Join
                      - ':root,arn:aws:iam::'
                      - !Ref AllowedAccountIds
            Action:
              - 'sqs:SendMessage'
            Resource: !GetAtt SecureEventQueue.Arn
            Condition:
              StringEquals:
                'aws:PrincipalAccount': !Ref AllowedAccountIds
          
          - Sid: AllowReceiveMessageFromAuthorizedAccounts
            Effect: Allow
            Principal:
              AWS: !Split
                - ','
                - !Sub
                  - 'arn:aws:iam::${inner}:root'
                  - inner: !Join
                      - ':root,arn:aws:iam::'
                      - !Ref AllowedAccountIds
            Action:
              - 'sqs:ReceiveMessage'
              - 'sqs:DeleteMessage'
              - 'sqs:GetQueueAttributes'
            Resource: !GetAtt SecureEventQueue.Arn
            Condition:
              StringEquals:
                'aws:PrincipalAccount': !Ref AllowedAccountIds
          
          - !If
            - HasVPCEndpoints
            - Sid: EnforceVPCEndpointAccess
              Effect: Deny
              Principal: '*'
              Action:
                - 'sqs:SendMessage'
                - 'sqs:ReceiveMessage'
              Resource: !GetAtt SecureEventQueue.Arn
              Condition:
                StringNotEquals:
                  'aws:SourceVpce': !Ref AllowedVPCEndpointIds
            - !Ref AWS::NoValue

  DeadLetterQueue:
    Type: AWS::SQS::Queue
    Properties:
      QueueName: !Sub '${Environment}-secure-event-queue-dlq'
      MessageRetentionPeriod: 1209600
      KmsMasterKeyId: !GetAtt QueueEncryptionKey.Arn
      Tags:
        - Key: Environment
          Value: !Ref Environment
        - Key: Purpose
          Value: DeadLetterQueue

  QueueDepthAlarm:
    Type: AWS::CloudWatch::Alarm
    Properties:
      AlarmName: !Sub '${Environment}-event-queue-depth-alarm'
      AlarmDescription: 'Alert when queue depth exceeds threshold'
      MetricName: ApproximateNumberOfMessagesVisible
      Namespace: AWS/SQS
      Statistic: Average
      Period: 300
      EvaluationPeriods: 2
      Threshold: 1000
      ComparisonOperator: GreaterThanThreshold
      Dimensions:
        - Name: QueueName
          Value: !GetAtt SecureEventQueue.QueueName

Outputs:
  QueueURL:
    Description: 'URL of the secure event queue'
    Value: !Ref SecureEventQueue
    Export:
      Name: !Sub '${Environment}-SecureEventQueueURL'
  
  QueueARN:
    Description: 'ARN of the secure event queue'
    Value: !GetAtt SecureEventQueue.Arn
    Export:
      Name: !Sub '${Environment}-SecureEventQueueARN'
  
  QueueName:
    Description: 'Name of the secure event queue'
    Value: !GetAtt SecureEventQueue.QueueName
  
  EncryptionKeyARN:
    Description: 'ARN of the KMS encryption key'
    Value: !GetAtt QueueEncryptionKey.Arn
  
  DeadLetterQueueARN:
    Description: 'ARN of the dead letter queue'
    Value: !GetAtt DeadLetterQueue.Arn
